title: Plugins
description: Tailwind v4 and typed HTTP client generation via @hushkey/howl/plugins

blocks.text: @hushkey/howl/plugins ships two build plugins: a Tailwind v4 integration and a typed HTTP client generator. Both run in the esbuild pipeline HowlBuilder drives — no Vite, no PostCSS config to hand-roll.
tailwindPlugin runs your CSS through PostCSS + Tailwind v4 at build time (minified in production). Attach it to a builder after constructing the HowlBuilder.
A tailwindConfigBase literal is also exported so a project tailwind.config.ts can extend Howl's defaults instead of starting from scratch.

httpClientGenPlugin crawls *.api.ts files and emits a typed HTTP client that mirrors your API paths as a nested object, a method per verb. Bodies, query and responses are typed from your Zod schemas, so a contract change is a compile error in every caller.

rows: Directory containing *.api.ts files (absolute or relative to cwd).
Where to write the generated client. The scaffolder uses ./generated/http-client.ts.
Import alias map for resolving schema imports referenced by your APIs.

blocks.text: The generated module exports http, shaped like your URL paths, ending in $get / $post / $put / $patch / $delete. Each takes an options object (params, query, headers, body) and resolves to { ok, status, ...body }.

The same generator is exposed as buildHttpClient(config) for one-shot generation outside the esbuild plugin (e.g. a codegen script). The plugin simply runs it on every build start; errors are logged but never abort the build.
